title: 1.1: Exports useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled from client/my-sites/plugins/hooks/use-is-marketplace-redesign-enabled.ts

annotations.description: Verifies that a function named useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled is exported from client/my-sites/plugins/hooks/use-is-marketplace-redesign-enabled.ts, taking no input, returning a boolean.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 348-389 create client/my-sites/plugins/hooks/use-is-marketplace-redesign-enabled.ts with `export function useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled(): boolean` at line 377. Interface specifies: Path client/my-sites/plugins/hooks/use-is-marketplace-redesign-enabled.ts, Name useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled, Type function, Input NA, Output boolean.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for 'a single centralized hook that captures all the visibility conditions.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies the exact path, name, input, output. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must export this hook from the specified path.

title: 1.2: Returns false from useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled when a site is selected

annotations.description: Verifies that useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled returns false whenever any site is currently selected, regardless of other conditions.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 383-384: `if ( siteId ) { return false; }` in useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled. Interface description states: 'Returns false when a site is selected.' Prompt states the redesign 'should only apply when no site is selected.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the redesign should only apply when no site is selected. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface description states: 'Returns false when a site is selected.' [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must return false from the hook when a site is selected.

title: 1.3: Returns true from useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled for logged-out users without a site selected

annotations.description: Verifies that useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled returns true for logged-out users when no site is selected, assuming the feature flag is enabled.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 388: `return isEnabled( 'marketplace-redesign' ) && ( ! isLoggedIn || hostingDashboardOptIn );` — when !isLoggedIn is true (logged out), the hook returns true. Interface description states: 'returns true only if the marketplace-redesign feature flag is enabled and the user is either logged out or has the hosting dashboard opt-in.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly includes logged-out users as a valid redesign context. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface description states the hook 'returns true only if the marketplace-redesign feature flag is enabled and the user is either logged out or has the hosting dashboard opt-in.' [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must allow the logged-out case through the hook.

title: 1.4: Returns true from useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled for hosting dashboard opt-in users without a site selected

annotations.description: Verifies that useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled returns true for logged-in users who have the hosting dashboard opt-in when no site is selected, assuming the feature flag is enabled.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 388: `return isEnabled( 'marketplace-redesign' ) && ( ! isLoggedIn || hostingDashboardOptIn );` — when hostingDashboardOptIn is true, the hook returns true. Interface description states: 'returns true only if the marketplace-redesign feature flag is enabled and the user is either logged out or has the hosting dashboard opt-in.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly includes hosting dashboard opt-in users as a valid context. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface description states the hook 'returns true only if the marketplace-redesign feature flag is enabled and the user is either logged out or has the hosting dashboard opt-in.' [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must allow the opt-in case through the hook.

title: 1.5: Returns false from useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled when the feature flag is disabled

annotations.description: Verifies that useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled returns false when the marketplace-redesign feature flag is disabled, even if no site is selected.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 388: `return isEnabled( 'marketplace-redesign' ) && ( ! isLoggedIn || hostingDashboardOptIn );` — when the feature flag is disabled, isEnabled returns false and the entire expression is false. Interface description states: 'returns true only if the marketplace-redesign feature flag is enabled.'
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The feature flag is the master rollout control; disabling it must disable the redesign entirely. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must respect the feature flag as a prerequisite for returning true.

title: 1.6: Adds an enabled prop to FullWidthSection at client/components/full-width-section/index.jsx

annotations.description: Verifies that FullWidthSection in client/components/full-width-section/index.jsx accepts an enabled prop of type boolean.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 17: `const FullWidthSection = ( { children, className, enabled = false } ) => {` adds the enabled parameter. gold.patch line 26: `enabled: PropTypes.bool` adds the prop type. Interface specifies: Input includes enabled: PropTypes.bool.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt says 'shared components like FullWidthSection that currently check the flag internally should be updated so the caller controls the redesign behavior.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies enabled: PropTypes.bool as an input. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must add the enabled prop to FullWidthSection.

title: 1.7: Defaults FullWidthSection to returning children without wrapping when enabled is omitted

annotations.description: Verifies that FullWidthSection returns children directly without the section wrapper when enabled is false or not provided. The default value of enabled must be false.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 17: `enabled = false` defaults the parameter. gold.patch line 18: `if ( ! enabled ) { return children; }` returns children directly when enabled is false. Interface description states: 'Returns children directly without wrapping when enabled is false. Defaults enabled to false.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt says shared components like FullWidthSection should be updated so the caller controls the redesign behavior. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies that enabled defaults to false, producing a passthrough. [3: Logical Necessity] Without defaulting to false, existing callers that do not pass enabled would break. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must default FullWidthSection to passthrough behavior.

title: 1.8: Accepts an isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled parameter in getCategories at client/my-sites/plugins/categories/use-categories.tsx

annotations.description: Verifies that getCategories in client/my-sites/plugins/categories/use-categories.tsx accepts an isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled boolean parameter, defaulting to false.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 158-160: `export const getCategories = ( isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled = false ): Record< string, Category > => ({` adds the boolean parameter with default false. Interface specifies: Input isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled: boolean, Output Record<string, Category>.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt says 'utility functions like getCategories that currently check the flag internally should be updated so the caller controls the redesign behavior.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies the parameter name, type, default. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must add this parameter to getCategories.

title: 1.9: Varies all 9 getCategories category fields (paid title, paid description, popular title, featured title, featured description, monetization title, monetization description, business title, business description) based on the isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled parameter

annotations.description: Verifies that calling getCategories with isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled set to true produces different values for all 9 flag-dependent fields (paid title, paid description, popular title, featured title, featured description, monetization title, monetization description, business title, business description) than calling it with false.
annotations.evidence: Interface description states: 'Uses the isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled parameter to determine alternate titles and descriptions for categories like paid, popular, featured, monetization, and business.' gold.patch lines 168-228 show all 9 isEnabled('marketplace-redesign') ternaries replaced with the parameter across these categories.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt says utility functions like getCategories that currently check the flag internally should be updated so the caller controls the redesign behavior. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies that the parameter controls alternate category content. [3: Logical Necessity] If the parameter is accepted but not used, the refactor has no effect on category display. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must vary all 9 category fields based on the parameter.

title: 1.10: Replaces all 14 pre-existing direct isEnabled('marketplace-redesign') call sites (full-width-section/index.jsx, categories/index.tsx, use-categories.tsx, education-footer/index.tsx, plugin-details-header/index.jsx, plugin-details.jsx, plugins-browser-item/index.jsx, collection-list-view/index.tsx, plugins-browser/index.jsx, single-list-view/index.jsx, plugins-category-results-page/index.jsx, plugins-discovery-page/index.jsx, plugins-search-results-page/index.jsx, related-plugins/index.tsx) with useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled

annotations.description: Verifies that all 14 component files that previously called isEnabled('marketplace-redesign') directly now consume useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled or receive the redesign state via a prop or parameter instead. Note: client/dashboard/plugins/plugins-menu/index.tsx is excluded because it is a dashboard component that did not previously contain an isEnabled('marketplace-redesign') call — the gold.patch intentionally introduces a new direct isEnabled call there (covered by rubric 1.13).
annotations.evidence: Prompt states: 'Every component in the plugins area that currently calls isEnabled("marketplace-redesign") directly should use this hook instead.' gold.patch lines show isEnabled('marketplace-redesign') replaced across full-width-section/index.jsx, categories/index.tsx, use-categories.tsx, education-footer/index.tsx, plugin-details-header/index.jsx, plugin-details.jsx, plugins-browser-item/index.jsx, collection-list-view/index.tsx, plugins-browser/index.jsx, single-list-view/index.jsx, plugins-category-results-page/index.jsx, plugins-discovery-page/index.jsx, plugins-search-results-page/index.jsx, and related-plugins/index.tsx. The dashboard plugins menu (client/dashboard/plugins/plugins-menu/index.tsx) is not one of these 14 files — it gains a new isEnabled call intentionally.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly requires all direct isEnabled calls in the plugins area to be replaced with the hook. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The dashboard plugins menu is in client/dashboard/, outside the plugins area scope, and intentionally uses isEnabled directly. [3: Logical Necessity] Creating the hook is insufficient if existing plugins-area components bypass it. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must migrate all 14 pre-existing direct flag checks to useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled across the plugins area.

title: 1.11: Converts both static module-level flag-dependent constants (COLLECTION_LIST_LENGTH in collection-list-view/index.tsx, SHORT_LIST_LENGTH in single-list-view/index.jsx) into runtime computations

annotations.description: Verifies that both module-scope constants COLLECTION_LIST_LENGTH (in collection-list-view/index.tsx) and SHORT_LIST_LENGTH (in single-list-view/index.jsx) that were derived from isEnabled('marketplace-redesign') at import time are now computed inside components at render time so they respond to useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled.
annotations.evidence: Prompt states: 'Static module level constants that depend on the flag should become dynamic so they respond to the hook at runtime.' gold.patch line 582 removes COLLECTION_LIST_LENGTH from module scope in collection-list-view/index.tsx; line 707 removes SHORT_LIST_LENGTH from module scope in single-list-view/index.jsx.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly asks for static flag-dependent constants to become dynamic. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Module-scope constants cannot access React hook values. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must move both COLLECTION_LIST_LENGTH and SHORT_LIST_LENGTH into the component body so they respond to useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled at runtime.

title: 1.12: Enables the marketplace-redesign feature flag in the config

annotations.description: Verifies that the marketplace-redesign feature flag is set to true in the relevant configuration file.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch line 1004: `"marketplace-redesign": true` in config/wpcalypso.json changes the flag from false to true. Prompt states: 'Finally, enable the marketplace-redesign flag in the production config so the new conditional logic takes effect.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly asks to enable the flag. [3: Logical Necessity] Without enabling the flag, the hook always returns false, making the refactor inert. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must set the flag to true.

title: 1.13: Adapts the Browse plugins link rendering in client/dashboard/plugins/plugins-menu/index.tsx based on the marketplace-redesign feature flag

annotations.description: Verifies that the plugins menu at client/dashboard/plugins/plugins-menu/index.tsx conditionally changes how the 'Browse plugins' link is rendered depending on whether the marketplace-redesign feature flag is enabled.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 30-76: client/dashboard/plugins/plugins-menu/index.tsx adds `isEnabled('marketplace-redesign')` and conditionally renders a Button (when enabled) vs a ResponsiveMenu.Item with target='_blank' (when disabled) for the 'Browse plugins' link.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt says 'The plugins menu should also adapt its rendering of the Browse plugins link based on whether the feature.' [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must make the plugins menu render the Browse plugins link differently based on the feature flag state.

title: 4.1: Adds an isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled parameter to getFirstCategoryFromTags in client/my-sites/plugins/categories/use-categories.tsx

annotations.description: Detects whether the solution adds an isMarketplaceRedesignEnabled parameter to getFirstCategoryFromTags in client/my-sites/plugins/categories/use-categories.tsx. The interface only specifies getCategories as the function to parameterize; getFirstCategoryFromTags is a separate exported function whose signature should remain unchanged.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch does not modify the signature of getFirstCategoryFromTags. The interface lists only three APIs to modify: useIsMarketplaceRedesignEnabled, FullWidthSection, and getCategories. Prompt states: 'Your task is to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt says to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies getCategories as the only function in use-categories.tsx to parameterize; getFirstCategoryFromTags is a separate export with its own callers. [3: Logical Necessity] Adding a parameter to getFirstCategoryFromTags forces all its callers to be updated, expanding the refactor scope beyond what was requested. [5: Conclusion] A correct solution must not add parameters to getFirstCategoryFromTags.
